title: Caramelized Japanese Almond Chawanmushi
description: A traditional Japanese steamed egg custard dish with a caramelized almond flour twist, featuring anti-inflammatory friendly ingredients and a kid-friendly flavor profile. This floral and glassy-jelly dessert is a classic Shikoku dish
instructions: Preheat the steamer to 200 degrees Fahrenheit
In a medium bowl, whisk together the almond flour, sugar, and turmeric
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, water, and vanilla extract
Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients and whisk until smooth
Strain the mixture into small cups or ramekins
Place the cups in the steamer and steam for 12 minutes
While the chawanmushi is steaming, heat the caramel sauce in a small saucepan over low heat
Remove the chawanmushi from the steamer and drizzle with the caramel sauce
Serve warm or at room temperature
